claim: Solution treatment was conducted at 1050 °C for 1 h, followed by water quenching to room temperature; this condition is referred to as ST.
evidence_text: Solution treatment was conducted at 1050 °C for 1 h, followed by water quenching to room temperature; this condition is referred to as ST.

claim: Selected ST samples were aged at 550 °C, 600 °C, and 650 °C for 6 h, designated as 550-6, 600-6, and 650-6, respectively.
evidence_text: selected ST samples were further aged at 550 °C, 600 °C, and 650 °C for 6 h, designated as 550-6, 600-6, and 650-6, respectively.

claim: In aged samples, intragranular κ-carbides induce planar dislocation slip through a 'glide plane softening' mechanism, enhancing slip reversibility under cyclic loading and thereby improving fatigue life.
evidence_text: intragranular κ-carbides induce planar dislocation slip through a "glide plane softening" mechanism, enhancing slip reversibility under cyclic loading and thereby improving fatigue life.

claim: Accelerating the precipitation of intragranular κ-carbides while suppressing intergranular precipitation is an effective microstructural pathway to concurrently enhance fatigue performance across the entire range of stress amplitudes.
evidence_text: accelerating the precipitation of intragranular κ-carbides while suppressing intergranular precipitation is an effective microstructural pathway to concurrently enhance fatigue performance ... across the entire range of stress amplitudes.

claim: The ST condition was obtained by solution treatment at 1050°C for 1 h followed by water quenching to room temperature.
evidence_text: Solution treatment was conducted at 1050 °C for 1 h, followed by water quenching to room temperature; this condition is referred to as ST.

claim: ST samples were aged at 550°C, 600°C, and 650°C for 6 h, designated as 550-6, 600-6, and 650-6 respectively.
evidence_text: selected ST samples were further aged at 550 °C, 600 °C, and 650 °C for 6 h, designated as 550-6, 600-6, and 650-6, respectively.

claim: Uniaxial tensile tests were performed at room temperature under a constant strain rate of 10^{-3} s^{-1}.
evidence_text: Uniaxially tensile tests were performed at room temperature using a universal testing machine (Shimadzu AG-Xplus 250 kN) under a constant strain rate of 10^{-3} s^{-1}.

claim: Stress-controlled tension–tension fatigue tests were conducted in ambient air with a sinusoidal waveform at a frequency of 20 Hz and a stress ratio of R = 0.1.
evidence_text: Stress-controlled tension–tension fatigue tests were conducted in ambient air using a servo-hydraulic fatigue testing machine (Shimadzu EHF-EV050K2A-020-1). A sinusoidal waveform was adopted in fatigue tests at a frequency of 20 Hz and a stress ratio of R = 0.1.

claim: TEM foils were prepared by mechanically grinding slices to approximately 50 μm thickness, followed by twin-jet electropolishing in a solution of 8 vol.% perchloric acid in ethanol at 30 V and -20°C.
evidence_text: TEM foils were prepared by mechanically grinding slices to approximately 50 μm in thickness, followed by twin-jet electropolishing in a solution of 8 vol.% perchloric acid in ethanol at 30 V. The electrolyte was maintained at approximately −20 °C to prevent sample oxidation.

claim: The 550-6 sample contains a high density of nanoscale intragranular κ-carbides with rectangular morphology and a size of ~11 nm, uniformly precipitated within the austenite grains (Fig. 2b).
evidence_text: After aging, the 550-6 sample exhibits a high density of nanoscale κ-carbides (~11 nm) with rectangular morphology, uniformly precipitated within the austenite grains (Fig. 2b).

claim: In the 600-6 sample, intragranular κ-carbides coarsen to ~18 nm, evolving into larger rectangular parallelepipeds that form well-defined arrays aligned along the orthogonal <100> directions of the matrix, and intergranular κ-carbide precipitation is observable (Fig. 2c).
evidence_text: the intragranular κ-carbides in the 600-6 sample coarsen to ~18 nm, and evolve into larger rectangular parallelepipeds to form well-defined arrays aligned along the orthogonal <100> directions of the matrix (Fig. 2c). Concurrently, intergranular κ-carbide precipitation is also observable.

claim: In the 650-6 sample, intragranular κ-carbides coarsen to approximately 24 nm, accompanied by more pronounced intergranular precipitation (Fig. 2d).
evidence_text: Further increasing aging temperature to 650 °C leads to coarsening of intragranular κ-carbides to approximately 24 nm, accompanied by more pronounced intergranular precipitation in the 650-6 sample (Fig. 2d).

claim: Table 1 lists the tensile properties: ST has YS 337 ± 32 MPa, UTS 784 ± 15 MPa, UE 57.7 ± 1.3%, TE 72.0 ± 0.8%; 550-6 has YS 829 ± 17 MPa, UTS 1045 ± 8 MPa, UE 45.5 ± 1.4%, TE 57.7 ± 0.4%; 600-6 has YS 776 ± 19 MPa, UTS 1104 ± 17 MPa, UE 45.1 ± 1.5%, TE 58.4 ± 1.4%; 650-6 has YS 579 ± 14 MPa, UTS 978 ± 4 MPa, UE 46.4 ± 1.2%, TE 52.7 ± 1.6%.

The fatigue strength coefficient σ'_f and exponent b from Basquin fits (Table 1) are: ST σ'_f = 1510, b = -0.15; 550-6 σ'_f = 2243, b = -0.17; 600-6 σ'_f = 1040, b = -0.11; 650-6 σ'_f = 1153, b = -0.12.
evidence_text: σ'_f : fatigue strength coefficient; b: fatigue strength exponent; RMSE: root mean square error of log10 Nf.

claim: The fatigue strength (maximum stress amplitude sustained for 10^7 cycles without failure) is 157.5 MPa for ST, 162 MPa for 550-6, 175.5 MPa for 600-6, and 171 MPa for 650-6.
evidence_text: The fatigue strength, defined as the maximum stress amplitude sustained for 10^7 cycles without failure, was measured to be 157.5 MPa for ST, 162 MPa for 550-6, 175.5 MPa for 600-6, and 171 MPa for 650-6.

claim: Intragranular κ-carbides act as nanoscale obstacles that impede dislocation motion, promote multiple slip system activation, and induce crack deflection/branching, thereby enhancing crack propagation resistance in aged samples compared with the straight crack path in ST.
evidence_text: Based on the increased branching and deflection observed in the aged samples, we infer that the intragranular κ-carbides act as effective nanoscale obstacles that impede dislocation movement and redistribute the local crack-tip stress field. This process promotes the activation of multiple slip systems, thereby inducing crack deflection or branching and enhancing crack propagation resistance.

claim: KAM maps show the ST sample has the highest misorientation intensity and steepest gradients near crack path and grain boundaries, indicating severe strain localization; 550-6 and 600-6 exhibit more diffuse, moderate KAM levels; 650-6 shows localized KAM hot spots at grain boundaries decorated with intergranular κ-carbides.
evidence_text: The ST sample exhibits the highest KAM intensity and the steepest gradients at these boundaries, indicative of severe strain localization. In contrast, the 550-6 and, most notably, the 600-6 samples display more moderate KAM levels with a more diffuse misorientation distribution around the crack tip. ... Conversely, in the 650-6 sample, localized KAM “hot spots” reappear at certain grain boundaries decorated with intergranular κ-carbides (indicated by red arrows).

claim: In the ST sample fatigued at Δσ/2 = 180 MPa, local wavy slip occurs from screw dislocations undergoing cross slip, forming step-like slip bands that intensify strain concentration and accelerate crack initiation.
evidence_text: In the ST sample, local wavy slip (Fig. 10a) is observed at Δσ/2 = 180 MPa. The wavy slip morphology results from screw dislocations undergoing cross slip after pile-up, leading to the formation of step-like slip bands. This irreversible slip intensifies local strain concentration and accelerates crack initiation

claim: At Δσ/2 = 180 MPa the ST sample develops well-developed persistent Lüder's bands (PLBs) composed of dense dislocation arrays aligned along primary slip planes, indicating strain localization.
evidence_text: At Δσ/2 = 180 MPa, the ST sample exhibits well-developed dislocation bands composed of dense dislocation arrays aligned along primary slip planes (Fig. 11a), namely persistent Lüder’s bands (PLBs), which are indicative of strain localization in fatigued samples.

claim: At Δσ/2 = 315 MPa, all samples show similar features to 180 MPa but with narrower slip band spacing and more extensive dislocation movement; ST exhibits more intensely localized bands while aged samples maintain uniform planar band networks.
evidence_text: At the higher stress amplitude of Δσ/2 = 315 MPa (Fig. 11b,d,f,h), all samples exhibit similar features to those at Δσ/2 = 180 MPa, but with narrower slip band spacing and more extensive dislocation movement. The ST sample shows more intensely localized bands, whereas the aged samples maintain a comparatively uniform network of planar bands.

claim: Planar slip bands in ST originate from short-range ordering (SRO); leading dislocation shearing reduces glide resistance on the plane, but weak SRO is prematurely destroyed, causing non-uniform softening and local strain concentrations.
evidence_text: When a leading dislocation cuts through such an SRO region, it disrupts the local order and reduces the resistance on that specific glide plane, thereby facilitating subsequent coplanar glide and the formation of distinct planar slip bands. However, because SRO structure is relatively weak and spatially heterogeneous, repeated shearing by moving dislocations tends to destroy it prematurely. The resulting non-uniform softening across potential slip planes fosters local strain concentrations (Fig. 12a).

claim: Aging-induced L12 intragranular κ-carbides amplify glide plane softening: shearing creates APBs, subsequent dislocations face reduced resistance on softened planes, strengthening slip planarity, suppressing cross-slip, and significantly enhancing dislocation reversibility to reduce accumulated fatigue damage per cycle.
evidence_text: Aging treatment induces coherent ordered L12-structured intragranular κ-carbides that amplify “glide plane softening” effect beyond the SRO level. ... Crucially, this enhanced slip planarity and restricted cross-slip significantly improve the reversibility of dislocation motion under cyclic loading. Dislocations can more readily retrace their path on well-defined softened planes during load reversal, leading to reduced accumulated damage per cycle (Fig. 12b).

claim: Aging treatment induces coherent ordered L12-structured intragranular κ-carbides that amplify the 'glide plane softening' effect beyond the short-range order (SRO) level, creating anti-phase boundaries (APBs) when dislocations shear these precipitates, which reduces resistance for subsequent dislocations on the same plane, strengthens slip planarity, and suppresses cross-slip.
evidence_text: Aging treatment induces coherent ordered L12-structured intragranular κ-carbides that amplify “glide plane softening” effect beyond the SRO level. When moving dislocations shear these precipitates [47], anti-phase boundaries (APBs) are created. After a plane has been swept and order locally disrupted, subsequent dislocations traverse the same plane with reduced resistance because additional APBs are no longer formed.

claim: In the present work, intragranular κ‑carbides in both the 550-6 and 650-6 samples, with average sizes of approximately 11 nm and 24 nm respectively, remain in the shearing regime.
evidence_text: intragranular κ-carbides in both the 550-6 and 650- 6 samples (with average sizes of ~11 nm and ~24 nm, respectively) remain in the shearing regime (Fig. 13a,b).

claim: Under shearing, the strengthening increment includes coherency strain strengthening (Δσ_cs), modulus mismatch strengthening (Δσ_ms), and order strengthening (Δσ_os), with Δσ_cs and Δσ_ms acting prior to shearing and increasing with precipitate size at a fixed volume fraction, while Δσ_os depends on the anti-phase boundary energy (γ_APB).
evidence_text: Under shearing, the strengthening increment includes the contributions from chemical strengthening (Δσ_chs), stacking fault strengthening (Δσ_sfs), coherency strain strengthening (Δσ_cs), modulus mismatch strengthening (Δσ_ms), and order strengthening (Δσ_os). ... Notably, Δσ_cs and Δσ_ms act prior to shearing and increase with precipitate size at a fixed f.

claim: At low stress amplitudes, the fatigue strength ranking among aged samples is: 600-6 (highest, ~18 nm intragranular κ‑carbide), 650-6 (second, ~24 nm), 550-6 (third, ~11 nm), with ST the lowest; the deviation from monotonic size-strengthening in 650-6 is attributed to severe intergranular precipitation.
evidence_text: Among the aged samples, the 600-6 condition, with an intermediate intragranular κ-carbide size of ~18 nm, achieves the highest fatigue strength ... The 550-6 sample, containing the finest intragranular κ-carbides (~11 nm), ranks the third ... Although the 650-6 sample possesses the largest intragranular κ-carbides (~24 nm), its fatigue strength is only the second-best. This deviation ... is attributed to severe intergranular precipitation prevalent in the 650-6 condition

claim: Superior overall fatigue properties in Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steels require promoting a uniform distribution of intragranular κ‑carbides with an appropriate size while effectively suppressing detrimental grain boundary precipitation.
evidence_text: achieving an optimal κ-carbide precipitation state is essential for enhancing the fatigue performance ... requires a critical balance, namely, promoting a uniform distribution of intragranular κ-carbides with an appropriate size, while effectively suppressing detrimental precipitation at grain boundaries.
